message:
My friend Sarah texts me at least twice a year in a complete panic about something—a storm warning, a power outage rumor, you name it. Last week she called absolutely convinced we needed to 'prepare immediately' and I just... listened.

Then something shifted. Instead of tuning her out, I asked her what she actually *needed* to feel calmer. Turns out she just wanted to know her family had basics covered: water, a flashlight, important papers in one spot, a simple plan.

So we spent an afternoon doing exactly that—nothing dramatic, no bunker vibes. Just practical stuff. Now when the panic hits, she has something real to anchor to.

Honestly? I think the panic comes from *not knowing* you're ready, not from the emergencies themselves.

Do you have someone in your life like Sarah, or are you the worried one in the group? 😊
